untranslatable_reason: Signal requires 'implied volatility spike detection' and 'portfolio weight inverse to realized volatility', which are portfolio-level risk management mechanisms and derivatives-based concepts. Available indicators (hvol_20, india_vix) provide realized/spot volatility proxies only. The signal also specifies 'dynamic rebalancing based on volatility regime' which requires position-sizing logic (multiplying exposure by volatility factors) not expressible in entry/exit condition syntax. Implied volatility curve skew ('volatility smirk') detection is not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This signal describes a sophisticated volatility-managed portfolio strategy with dynamic position sizing—reducing notional exposure after vol spikes, increasing after vol compression. This is implementable as a risk management overlay (position size = base_size / volatility_ratio) but cannot be fully captured in condition-based entry/exit rules. Attempted translation uses hvol_20 as a proxy for realized volatility regime detection, but the core mechanism (inverse portfolio weight to volatility) requires external position-sizing logic outside the backtest condition framework.
